description:
## Goal

Every AST node carries the source span that produced it. The parser is the population point; no downstream pass invents a node without supplying a span (or, for synthesized nodes, a `SpanOrigin` ÔÇö see .20.3).

## Dependencies

- Requires .20.1 (Span / SourceMap / lexer byte offsets).

## Design

Two-tier strategy chosen to keep variant-matching readable:

### Wrapper for recursive types

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Spanned<T> {
    pub node: T,
    pub span: Span,
}
```

Used for `Expr` and `Pattern` ÔÇö every recursive position becomes `Box<Spanned<Expr>>` (or `Vec<Spanned<Expr>>` etc.). Rationale:

- Variant matching stays clean: `match &expr.node { Expr::Int(n) => ÔÇŽ }` rather than per-variant span fields.
- Equal cost: one `Span` per node either way.
- Standard pattern (rustc `ast::Expr` is `P<Expr> { id, kind: ExprKind, span, ÔÇŽ }`; this is the simplified form).

### Span field for fixed structs

`FnDef`, `FnClause`, `MatchClause`, `WithBinding`, `BitField`, `ModuleDef`, `Item` (the enum that holds module-level definitions) gain a `pub span: Span` field. No wrapper ÔÇö these aren't recursive in the same way, and the field is more ergonomic for the consumer.

`Program` has no top-level span (the whole file is the implicit span).

Operator nodes (`BinOp`, `UnOp`) currently sit inline inside `Expr::BinOp(BinOp, Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>)`. The wrapping `Spanned<Expr>` already gives the span of the whole expression; the operator-token span is rarely useful and adds noise. **Decision: do NOT add op-token spans.** If we later need them for "operand of `+` is wrong type, the `+` is here", we add a second secondary span at the diagnostic site by re-locating the operator from the parser (cheaper than carrying every operator span forever).

### Clone cost

`Expr` is widely cloned (resolve/macros do in-place rewrites with `e.clone()` in places). `Span` is 12 bytes `Copy` ÔÇö clone cost unchanged in practice. Verify no allocation regression in `cargo bench` (or `cargo build --timings` if no benches yet).

## What `src/ast.rs` looks like after

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum Expr {
    Int(i64),
    // ...
    List(Vec<Spanned<Expr>>, Option<Box<Spanned<Expr>>>),
    Tuple(Vec<Spanned<Expr>>),
    Call(Box<Spanned<Expr>>, Vec<Spanned<Expr>>),
    Dot(Box<Spanned<Expr>>, String),  // name is unspanned (it's a string slice, not a syntax node)
    BinOp(BinOp, Box<Spanned<Expr>>, Box<Spanned<Expr>>),
    If(Box<Spanned<Expr>>, Box<Spanned<Expr>>, Option<Box<Spanned<Expr>>>),
    Case(Box<Spanned<Expr>>, Vec<MatchClause>),
    // ...
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct MatchClause {
    pub pattern: Spanned<Pattern>,
    pub guard: Option<Spanned<Expr>>,
    pub body: Spanned<Expr>,
    pub span: Span,    // span of the entire clause: `pattern when guard, do: body`
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct FnClause {
    pub params: Vec<Spanned<Pattern>>,
    pub guard: Option<Spanned<Expr>>,
    pub body: Spanned<Expr>,
    pub span: Span,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct FnDef {
    pub name: String,
    pub name_span: Span,            // span of just the name token (for "redefinition of X")
    pub clauses: Vec<FnClause>,
    pub is_macro: bool,
    pub doc: Option<String>,
    pub span: Span,                  // span covering ALL clauses + @doc
}
```

Same pattern for `Item::Module { name_span, span, ÔÇŽ }`, `Item::Alias`, `Item::Import`, `Item::MacroCall`. Every name-position gets a `*_span` field where the name will later be reported in errors ("`X` was aliased here", "redefinition of `foo`").

## Parser changes

Every construction site in `src/parser.rs` (1168 lines, ~80 `Expr::ÔÇŽ(ÔÇŽ)` and `Pattern::ÔÇŽ(ÔÇŽ)` literals) gets wrapped. The pattern is:

```rust
let start = self.cur_span_start();
let node = Expr::BinOp(op, lhs, rhs);
let span = self.span_from(start);
Spanned { node, span }
```

Helpers to add on `Parser`:
- `cur_span_start(&self) -> u32` ÔÇö `self.toks[self.pos].span.start` (or end-of-input if past end).
- `span_from(&self, start: u32) -> Span` ÔÇö `Span { file, start, end: <last consumed token's span.end> }`.
- `peek_span(&self) -> Span` ÔÇö current token's span (for single-token nodes like `Expr::Int`).

For clauses / fn-defs / modules: parser already brackets them; just capture start at the opening keyword and end at the closing token (`end` for blocks, the last clause's body end for fn defs, etc.).

## Consumer updates (this ticket)

Every site that pattern-matches `Expr::ÔÇŽ` or `Pattern::ÔÇŽ` needs adjusting. Major sites:
- `src/resolve.rs::rewrite_expr` (845 lines) ÔÇö recurses Expr; just touch `e.node` instead of `*e`, propagate spans through `Box<Spanned<Expr>>` wrapping.
- `src/macros.rs::expand_expr` (629 lines) ÔÇö similar; the `*e = new_e` site at `macros.rs:256` needs new semantics (.20.3 covers this).
- `src/ir_lower.rs` (1829 lines) ÔÇö heaviest consumer; every `match e { ... }` becomes `match &e.node { ... }`. The lowering itself doesn't *use* the span yet (.20.4 wires that in), but the structural change happens here.
- `src/eval.rs` (interp; runs macros and the legacy interp path).
- `src/ast_value.rs` (Expr Ôćö Value encoding for `quote`).

This is the largest single ticket of the .20 arc by surface area. Suggest a single PR that compiles green at every commit so review is tractable.

## What about `Value`-encoded ASTs from macros?

`ast_value.rs::expr_to_value` / `value_to_expr` are the macro reification path. The Value-encoded form should **not** carry spans (Values are runtime data; spans are syntax-time metadata). When the macro returns and `value_to_expr` decodes back to AST, the resulting nodes get `Span::DUMMY`. .20.3 attaches `SpanOrigin` lineage to those at the *call site* of the macro, which is the right granularity.

## Tests

- Round-trip: parse a small program, walk the AST, verify every node's `span` slices to the source text it represents.
- The fixture `fixtures/modules.fz` is the canonical regression: parse it, walk, assert that the span of `Expr::Call(M.double, [21])` on line 11 covers exactly `print(M.double(21))`.

## Non-goals

- Semantic use of spans (.20.4 onward).
- Lineage for macro-generated nodes (.20.3).
- Operator-token sub-spans (see Design).
title: AST spans: Spanned<T> for Expr/Pattern, span fields for structs
